When children are under a mother's care, she has an opportunity to shape their minds correctly. Those who are grown up should often remember the good teaching they received when they were children. The many terrible examples of promising people ruined by immoral women and love of wine should warn everyone to avoid these evils. Wine is to be used for need or medicine. Every creature of God is good, and wine, though abused, has its use. By the same rule, praise and comfort should be used as encouragement for those who are discouraged and tempted, not given to those who are confident and self-sufficient. All in authority should be more carefully moderate even than other men; and should be protectors of those who are unable or afraid to plead their own cause. Our blessed Lord did not decline the bitterest part of the cup of sorrow put into his hands; but he puts the cup of comfort into the hands of his people, and causes those in deep distress to rejoice.

This is a description of a virtuous woman of those days, but the general qualities are suitable for every age and nation. She is very careful to gain her husband's respect and affection, to understand his mind, and is willing that he rule over her. 1. She can be trusted, and he will leave such a wife to manage things for him. He is happy with her. And she makes it her constant goal to do him good. 2. She takes care in her duties, and finds pleasure in them. She is careful to fill time so that none is wasted. She rises early. She applies herself to the work proper for her, women's work. She does what she does with all her power, and does not trifle. 3. She makes sure what she does benefits her by wise management. Many ruin themselves by buying things without considering whether they can afford them. She provides well for her house. She saves for the future. 4. She looks well to the ways of her household, so that she may encourage everyone to do their duty to God and one another, as well as to her. 5. She is intent on giving as on receiving, and does it freely and cheerfully. 6. She is discreet and helpful; every word she says shows that she governs herself by the rules of wisdom. She not only takes wise steps herself, but gives wise advice to others. The law of love and kindness is written in the heart, and shows itself in speech. Her heart is full of another world, even when her hands are most busy about this world. 7. Above all, she fears the Lord. Beauty does not recommend anyone to God, nor is it any proof of wisdom and goodness, but it has deceived many a man who chose a wife based on it. But the fear of God reigning in the heart is the beauty of the soul; it lasts forever. 8. She has strength to endure hardships and disappointments. She will reflect with comfort when she comes to be old that she was not idle or useless when young. She will rejoice in a world to come. She is a great blessing to her family. If the fruit is good, the tree deserves our praise. But she leaves it to her own works to praise her. Everyone ought to desire this honor that comes from God; and according to this standard we all ought to regulate our judgments. This description let all women daily study, who desire to be truly beloved and respected, useful and honorable. This passage is to be applied to individuals, but may it not also be applied to the church of God, which is described as a virtuous spouse? God by his grace has formed from among sinful men a church of true believers, to possess all the excellences here described.
